Analytics.title: Analytics
Analytics.description: Represents aggregated analytics data for a team or soldier.

id.description: Unique identifier for the analytics entry.

entityId.description: ID of the entity (team or soldier) this data pertains to.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the analytics data was generated.

Account.title: Account
Account.description: Represents a user account within the FitSquad application.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team this account belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N Account)

email.description: Email address associated with the account.

Team.title: Team
Team.description: Represents a team of soldiers, such as a section, squad, platoon, or company.

name.description: Name of the team (e.g., Section 1, Alpha Platoon).

teamCode.description: A user-friendly, 8-digit numeric code for joining the team.

SoldierData.title: SoldierData
SoldierData.description: Represents fitness and health data for a soldier.

id.description: Unique identifier for the soldier data entry.

accountId.description: Reference to the Account this data belongs to. (Relationship: Account 1:N SoldierData)

weight.description: Weight of the user in pounds.

height.description: Height of the user in inches.

restingHeartRate.description: Resting heart rate in beats per minute.

WorkoutPlan.title: WorkoutPlan
WorkoutPlan.description: Represents a workout plan tailored for a specific team.

id.description: Unique identifier for the workout plan.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team this workout plan is assigned to. (Relationship: Team 1:N WorkoutPlan)

startDate.description: Start date of the workout plan.

endDate.description: End date of the workout plan.

Exercise.title: Exercise
Exercise.description: Represents a specific exercise within a workout plan.

workoutPlanId.description: Reference to the WorkoutPlan this exercise belongs to. (Relationship: WorkoutPlan 1:N Exercise)

name.description: Name of the exercise (e.g., Push-ups, Squats).

sets.description: Number of sets for the exercise.

reps.description: Number of repetitions for each set.

goal.description: Specific goal for this exercise (e.g., Improve upper body strength).

Progress.title: Progress
Progress.description: Represents a soldier's progress on a specific exercise.

id.description: Unique identifier for the progress entry.

accountId.description: Reference to the Account who is tracking progress. (Relationship: Account 1:N Progress)

exerciseId.description: Reference to the Exercise this progress is for. (Relationship: Exercise 1:N Progress)

notes.description: Any notes on the exercise (e.g., felt easier today, increased weight).

date.description: Date when the progress was recorded.

definition.description: Stores user account information. Account data is stored under `/accounts/{accountId}` with path-based ownership.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user account.

definition.description: Stores teams a commander manages. This enables hierarchical data access.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user account (must be a commander).

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier for the managed team.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier for the team.

definition.description: stores the members of each team. Team membership is managed via the `/teams/{teamId}/members/{userId}` subcollection to grant roles. Each user must be represented as document to have access to the team content

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier for the team.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores fitness and health data for a soldier. Soldier data is nested under `/accounts/{accountId}/soldierData/{soldierDataId}`.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user account.

params.name: soldierDataId
params.description: The unique identifier for the soldier data.

definition.description: Stores workout plans for teams. Workout plans are stored under `/teams/{teamId}/workoutPlans/{workoutPlanId}`.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier for the team.

params.name: workoutPlanId
params.description: The unique identifier for the workout plan.

definition.description: Stores exercises for workout plans. Exercises related to a workout plan are nested under `/teams/{teamId}/workoutPlans/{workoutPlanId}/exercises/{exerciseId}`.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier for the team.

params.name: workoutPlanId
params.description: The unique identifier for the workout plan.

params.name: exerciseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exercise.

definition.description: Stores progress of exercises for soldiers. The progress of each soldier on each exercise is recorded under `/accounts/{accountId}/exercisesProgress/{progressId}`.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user account.

params.name: progressId
params.description: The unique identifier for the progress.

definition.description: Stores aggregated analytics data for a team.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier for the team.

params.name: analyticsId
params.description: The unique identifier for the analytics entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for FitSquad is designed to prioritize authorization independence and facilitate efficient data retrieval. It incorporates denormalization to avoid complex security rules involving `get()` calls. Path-based ownership is used for private user data, while membership maps are employed for collaborative data access. Segregation of data into collections with homogeneous security needs ensures simplified rule management and supports QAPs (Queries are not filters).

Specifically:

*   **Accounts:** Account data is stored under `/accounts/{accountId}` with path-based ownership.
*   **Teams:** Team data is stored under `/teams/{teamId}`. Team membership is managed via the `/teams/{teamId}/members/{userId}` subcollection to grant roles.
*   **SoldierData:** Soldier data is nested under `/accounts/{accountId}/soldierData/{soldierDataId}`. This structure ensures that soldier data is directly associated with the account, enabling path-based ownership security rules. This enforces privacy, ensures that each soldier can only update or view their data.
*   **WorkoutPlans:** Workout plans are stored under `/teams/{teamId}/workoutPlans/{workoutPlanId}`. This allows efficient retrieval of workout plans for a specific team.
*   **Exercises:** Exercises related to a workout plan are nested under `/teams/{teamId}/workoutPlans/{workoutPlanId}/exercises/{exerciseId}`.
*   **Progress:** The progress of each soldier on each exercise is recorded under `/accounts/{accountId}/exercisesProgress/{progressId}`.
*   **ManagedTeams:** For commanders, a subcollection at `/accounts/{accountId}/managedTeams/{teamId}` stores references to the teams they oversee, enabling a hierarchical view of data.

This structure enables simple and robust security rules because authorization context is denormalized into each document, avoiding complex `get()` calls. Collection segregation ensures homogeneous security postures, further simplifying rules and supporting QAPs.
